summary: R2 systems architecture review cannot accept M4 withdrawal/postwar handoff yet. The R2 fixes address source-ledger grain return, WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate deep-copying, and failed-extraction coverage, and all required gates passed locally. However, objective-complete still does not require a march reference before creating a WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate, so a completed campaign with a surrendered siege can be handed off with payload.marchId null and bypass the required siege/march match. The new return-ledger ordering also lacks a unique stable source tie-breaker for multi-source reservation rows.

tests_run.result: On feat/m4-withdrawal-postwar-handoff with R2 worktree changes in packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts, tests/sim-core-m4-withdrawal-postwar-handoff.test.ts, allowed coordination state, and untracked REVIEW-R2 handoff.
Vitest passed: 1 file, 10 tests. Existing tests do not cover objective-complete with payload.marchId null.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 32 files and 222 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file and 3 tests passed.
Task JSON validated successfully in REVIEW state.
Writer REVIEW-R2 handoff JSON validated successfully.

decisions: Keep REQUEST_CHANGES because prior blocker 1 requires objective-complete to match a completed campaign result to both target and march, but validateObjectiveCompleteHandoffResult accepts march null.
Accept the R2 direction for source-ledger grain return, read-model deep-copying, and failed-extraction coverage, subject to the deterministic ledger tie-breaker cleanup below.

No hard-exclusion implementation was found in the R2 diff: no full diplomacy, peace treaties, rebellion system, formal historical content, campaign planning UI, M5 content, manual node battle, complex naval warfare, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, save-format package change, or parallel postwar settlement model.

risks.issue: validateObjectiveCompleteHandoffResult checks siege/march equality only when input.march is non-null. Because evaluateResolveM4CampaignWithdrawal passes march null when payload.marchId is null, objective-complete can still create a WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate without an explicit march reference matching the surrendered siege result.

compareM4GrainReservationForReturnLedger returns only reservationId. Grain reservations may contain multiple rows for the same reservationId from different sources, and partial return allocation can then depend on existing array order instead of a unique stable source tie-breaker.

blockers: For objective-complete, require payload.marchId to resolve to a march and require siege.marchId === march.marchId before creating WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate; add a regression proving objective-complete with marchId null is rejected and does not create outcomes/candidates.

Update compareM4GrainReservationForReturnLedger to include a unique stable source tie-breaker such as source kind, districtId, populationGroupId, and then reservation quantity/status fields if needed; add or extend a test with multiple source rows sharing a reservationId where returned grain is allocated deterministically.
requested_action: Fix the remaining objective-complete march-reference gate and deterministic ledger ordering issue, rerun the required M4-WITHDRAWAL-POSTWAR-HANDOFF-001 gates, and return REVIEW-R3 to systems_architect.
